subject: M may not give expected results
body:
Hi -

 I have been trying to run M (1.1.0 beta)  on a windows station with 4 RTX 
3090 GPUs (cuda 12.2), but the sub-tomograms generated lead to a refinement 
with worse resolution than before going through M. 
 Looking more into the star file generated, I noticed the X,Y,Z coordinates 
of the sub-tomograms were changing dramatically. I would expect the 
coordinate in the output file to be equal to something like
"(_rlnCoordinateX - _rlnOriginXAngst x PixelSize) x scaling_factor"  

with scaling factor the difference between the pixelsize of the star file 
given to M and the one generated after refinement in M. 

 To try to debug, I imported a star file, and then immediately exported 
exported sub-tomograms without refinement at the same pixel size as the 
input one. I noticed the coordinates of the first sub-tomogram was as 
expected, but all the others I checked had X,Y,Z, coodinates which were 
significantly different from those in the input file. 

 Has anyone any idea? I've seen other threads mentioning various issues 
with RTX 3090, so I was wondering if it could be some configuration / 
compatibility issue? Shall we downgrade to an older version of cuda (if so, 
which one would you advise)?

Thanks,
Nicolas
